title: Pentanema × savii
taxonomy: Pentanema × savii is a hybrid species within the genus Pentanema, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is a cross between two different species of Pentanema, resulting in a unique and distinct taxon. The classification of Pentanema × savii is primarily based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with its parent species. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the specific criteria used. Pentanema × savii is typically classified as a hybrid or a cultivar within the broader context of the Asteraceae family.
uses: Pentanema × savii has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its leaves and flowers reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Studies on Pentanema × savii suggest that it may help in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pentanema × savii for medicinal purposes. Further investigation is necessary to unlock the full potential of this plant in modern medicine.

constituents: Pentanema × savii contains a range of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, and flavonoids. These active constituents contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and may provide antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. Research has identified several specific compounds, such as 8-deoxylartigenin and arnicolide, which have been isolated from Pentanema × savii. intro: Pentanema × savii, also known as Savii's pentanema or Savii's knapweed, is a hybrid perennial plant species of the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in treating gastrointestinal disorders and relieving pain. The bioactive constituents of Pentanema × savii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues and as a natural anti-inflammatory remedy.
